home.coverPromise: Know what a game does to your kid — before they play.

home.h1: A great game can enrich a growing mind. The wrong one needs a grown-up.
home.subhead: LumiKin rates {count} games on benefits, risks, and healthy play time, so you can tell which is which.
home.searchPlaceholder: Search a game your kid plays…
home.featuredEyebrow: How a LumiKin rating is built
home.featuredIntro: One game, three steps: what it is, what we found in it, and what we recommend.
home.featuredMeterCognitiveTitle: Problem solving, strategy, creativity, memory — the B1 group
home.featuredMeterSocialTitle: Teamwork, communication, empathy, ethical reasoning — the B2 group
home.featuredMeterMotorTitle: Hand-eye, fine motor, reaction time, physical activity — the B3 group
home.featuredMeterDopamineTitle: Variable rewards, streaks, FOMO, near-miss — the R1 group
home.featuredMeterMonetizationTitle: Pay-to-win, currency obfuscation, child-targeting — the R2 group
home.featuredMeterSocialRiskTitle: Stranger interaction, toxicity, identity pressure — the R3 group
home.tile1Body: We're a gaming-positive site. Every rating leads with what a game actually builds — problem solving, teamwork, creativity — before any risks.
home.tile2Body: Each game gets a recommended session length, derived from how the game is built — not a generic screen-time rule.
home.tile3Body: Scores come from a public rubric covering 60 sub-dimensions. You can read exactly why a game got the rating it did.
home.methodologyIntro: Every game is scored on developmental benefits and design risks across 60 sub-dimensions from a public rubric. The two scores combine into a recommended session length — from 15 minutes to two hours — that reflects how the game is built, not a generic limit.
home.methodologyDetailsBenefits: Benefits cover ten cognitive dimensions (problem solving, spatial awareness, strategic thinking, creativity, and more), six social-emotional dimensions (teamwork, communication, empathy, ethical reasoning), and four motor dimensions — weighted into a <bds>Benefits Development Score (BDS)</bds>.
home.methodologyDetailsRisks: Risks are scored across dopamine manipulation design (<variable>variable rewards</variable>, <streaks>streaks</streaks>, <fomo>FOMO triggers</fomo>), monetization pressure (<paytowin>pay-to-win</paytowin>, <currency>currency obfuscation</currency>, child-targeting), and social risk (stranger interaction, toxic competition, identity pressure) — combined into a <ris>Risk Influence Score (RIS)</ris>.
home.businessPartnersBody: Child-safety ratings API for parental control vendors, ISPs, and platforms.
home.businessPressBody: Coverage stats, methodology, press kit, and contact info.
home.librarySellHeading: Track what your kid actually plays.
home.librarySellSubhead: Sign up free to build a per-child library, sync from Steam, and stay in the loop when a game's rating changes.
home.librarySellTile1Body: Add the games each child owns or wants, and see ratings filtered to their age, platforms, and focus skills.
home.librarySellTile2Body: Pull a whole Steam library in one click — no manual entry. Epic and Nintendo coming next.
home.librarySellTile3Body: Get a heads-up when a game your family plays gets re-rated — after an update, a new monetization layer, or a fresh review.
home.librarySellTile4Body: One account, one profile per kid. Each gets their own age, platforms, and skill focus — and their own library view.
home.librarySellReassure: Free forever for parents. No card required.
home.metaTitle: Game ratings for parents — Age, benefits & time per session | LumiScore
home.metaDescription: Find safe games for kids. LumiScore shows what each game teaches, what to watch for, and a healthy daily time limit. {count}+ games reviewed.
home.faq1Answer: LumiScore is LumiKin's 0–100 rating for every game. It combines a Benefits Development Score (skills the game builds) with a Risk Influence Score (addictive mechanics, monetization pressure, social risk) into a single number plus a recommended daily session length.
home.faq2Answer: Every game is scored across 60 sub-dimensions from a public rubric — ten cognitive skills, six social-emotional skills, four motor skills on the benefits side, and dopamine, monetization, and social-risk design patterns on the risk side. Read the full methodology for the formulas.
home.faq3Question: How do you decide how long my child should play?
home.faq3Answer: We calculate a daily session limit from the combined Benefits and Risk scores — from 15 minutes for high-risk games to two hours for high-benefit, low-risk ones. The limit reflects how the game is built, not a generic screen-time recommendation.
home.methodologySource: Built on a public rubric — version-controlled and free to download.

discover.statUseMonetization: use loot boxes or in-game purchases
discover.searchPlaceholder: Search a game your child plays…
discover.heading: Discover Games
discover.zoneCautionDesc: Design risks outweigh benefits. Limit sessions carefully.
discover.zoneModerateDesc: Mixed profile. Worth a closer look before regular play.
discover.zoneGreatDesc: Strong benefits, manageable risks. More daily time approved.
discover.swapRiskMonetization: This game uses real-money purchases, loot boxes, or currency obfuscation — design techniques proven to encourage overspending, especially in children.
discover.swapRiskDopamine: This game is engineered with variable reward loops and near-miss mechanics — the same psychological patterns used in slot machines — to make it hard to stop playing.
discover.swapRiskSocial: This game uses social obligation mechanics: guild requirements, friend leaderboards, and competitive pressure that create anxiety around logging off.
discover.swapRiskGeneral: Our analysis found multiple high-risk design patterns that outweigh its developmental benefits.
discover.swapFromHeavyMon: Heavy monetization pressure and spending prompts
discover.swapFromSocial: Unmoderated social features and stranger-interaction risks
discover.swapFromDopamine: Compulsive loop design — hard to put down
discover.swapFromGeneral: High overall risk score across safety dimensions
discover.swapToStrong: Strong developmental benefits with minimal safety concerns
discover.swapToSimilar: Similar gameplay energy with meaningful skills development
discover.swapToSameGenre: Same genre appeal — no spending pressure or social risks
discover.swapToSafer: Much safer by the numbers, comparable fun factor
discover.metaDescription: Find the right game for your child, grounded in child development.

gameCard.ratingPendingSub: This game hasn't been reviewed yet.
gameCard.scoringPending: Detailed scoring is available once a review is submitted.
gameCard.adversarialDebate: Adversarial debate · {rounds, plural, one {# round} other {# rounds}}
gameCard.risMinimal: Minimal pressure to spend or play excessively.
gameCard.risNotable: Notable design patterns that encourage extended play.
gameCard.virtualCurrencySub: real costs may not be obvious to children
gameCard.contentRiskNote: Content risk is displayed separately and does not affect the time recommendation.
gameCard.dp05Message: 🧸 Characters in this game directly ask players to make purchases
gameCard.riskNotesDopamine: Variable rewards, streaks, FOMO events, and other engagement mechanics
gameCard.riskNotesMonetization: In-app purchases, pay-to-win elements, and spending prompts
gameCard.riskNotesSocial: Social obligation, competitive toxicity, stranger interaction
gameCard.riskNotesContent: Violence, language, and other content factors — context depends on age
gameCard.tooltipCognitive: Problem solving, spatial awareness, strategic thinking, creativity, memory, and learning transfer. Weighted 50% of the Benefit Score.
gameCard.tooltipSocialEmotional: Teamwork, communication, empathy, emotional regulation, and ethical reasoning. Weighted 30% of the Benefit Score.
gameCard.tooltipMotor: Hand-eye coordination, fine motor control, reaction time, and physical activity. Weighted 20% of the Benefit Score.
gameCard.tooltipDopamine: Variable rewards, streaks, FOMO events, near-miss mechanics, and other design patterns that exploit reward psychology.
gameCard.tooltipMonetization: In-app purchases, pay-to-win mechanics, virtual currency obfuscation, spending prompts, and ad pressure.
gameCard.tooltipSocialRisk: Social obligations, competitive toxicity, stranger interaction, social comparison, and privacy concerns.
gameCard.tooltipContent: Violence, language, sexual content, and other age-related content factors. Shown separately — does not affect the time recommendation.
gameCard.tooltipRepresentation: How diverse the game's characters are in gender and ethnicity. Higher = more authentic representation. Display only — does not affect time recommendation.
gameCard.tooltipIdeological: Presence of propaganda, nationalist framing, or strong ideological content. 0=neutral, 3=heavy. Display only — does not affect time recommendation.
gameCard.debateDescription: Two AI models debated this score in {rounds, plural, one {# round} other {# rounds}}: an Advocate arguing for the highest defensible scores, and a Critic arguing for the lowest. The final score averages their round-2 positions.
gameCard.bechdelTooltip: The Bechdel Test checks whether a game has at least two named female characters who talk to each other about something other than a man. A simple measure of representation.
gameCard.bundledOnlinePreamble: The LumiScore above reflects the single-player campaign only.

darkPatterns.heading: Detected Tactics
darkPatterns.dp01Desc: Low-cost starter pack normalizes spending before expensive offers appear
darkPatterns.dp02Desc: Guilting language on decline buttons ("Are you sure you don't want to be cool?")
darkPatterns.dp03Desc: Fake countdown timers or "limited stock" on unlimited digital goods
darkPatterns.dp04Desc: Real money converted to gems/crystals/coins, reducing spending awareness
darkPatterns.dp05Desc: In-game character the child has bonded with asks them to buy things
darkPatterns.dp06Desc: Streak mechanics that punish missed days rather than just rewarding attendance
darkPatterns.dp07Desc: Stamina/energy system that refills on timer or purchase
darkPatterns.dp08Desc: Progress requires friends (gift exchanges, raid parties, co-op gates)
darkPatterns.dp09Desc: Random-chance paid rewards with undisclosed or poor odds
darkPatterns.dp10Desc: Paywalled shortcuts past intentionally tedious progression
darkPatterns.dp12Desc: Time-limited content/events designed to create urgency

compliance.heading: Regulatory Compliance
compliance.footer: Tap a badge for details. Grey = not yet assessed.
compliance.dsaDesc: Meets transparency requirements for minors under the EU Digital Services Act.
compliance.gdprkDesc: Proper consent and age-gating in place for users under 16 (GDPR Article 8).
compliance.oddsDesc: Publishes drop rates for paid random rewards (required in CN, JP, KR).

roblox.title: Roblox
roblox.subtitle: AI safety ratings for Roblox experiences
roblox.ratingInProgressDesc: Our AI is evaluating this experience. Check back soon.
roblox.metaDescVerdict: {parts}. Parent verdict and risk breakdown for {title} on Roblox.
roblox.metaTitleFallback: {title} on Roblox — Safe for kids? | LumiKin
roblox.metaDescFallback: LumiKin safety rating for {title} on Roblox — benefits, risks, and screen time guidance for parents.
roblox.scoringNote: This experience is scored on 9 dimensions adapted from the {count}-dimension LumiKin rubric. Risk category weights match the rubric (Dopamine 45%, Monetization 30%, Social 25%); per-category sub-items are aggregated into a single score.
roblox.lowConfidenceLong: This experience doesn't yet have enough public info for us to rate it confidently. We'll update as more data arrives.
roblox.parentGuidePara1: By default, Roblox allows unfiltered chat and friend requests from strangers. Enable Account Restrictions immediately — it takes 2 minutes and makes the platform significantly safer for children under 13.
roblox.parentGuidePara2: Roblox is a platform of 40 million+ user-made games, not a single game. Quality, safety, and age-appropriateness vary dramatically between experiences. The LumiKin ratings above reflect individual experiences — the platform itself does not guarantee safety.
roblox.parentGuidePara3: Robux is the in-game currency used across most popular experiences. Many games are designed around Robux spending — pay-to-win mechanics, exclusive cosmetics, and social comparison of avatar items are common. Set a clear spending policy before your child encounters the first purchase prompt.
roblox.parentGuidePara4: The chat filter is imperfect. Children regularly find workarounds (number substitutions, deliberate misspellings). Monitor chat history periodically using the Parent PIN tools, and have an open conversation about what to do if someone says something uncomfortable.
roblox.parentGuideAction: Action: Roblox settings → Privacy → Account Restrictions (ON). This restricts chat, friend requests, and game access to a pre-screened age-appropriate set. Set a Parent PIN to prevent your child from disabling it.
roblox.hubMetaDescription: LumiKin ratings for popular Roblox experiences. Find out which games are safe for your child, with scores for stranger risk, monetization pressure, and more.
roblox.hubTagline: Millions of user-generated experiences — safety varies widely. Browse our ratings to find the best fits for your child.
roblox.noIndexed: No experiences indexed yet. Check back soon.

fortnite.title: Fortnite Creative
fortnite.subtitle: AI safety ratings for Fortnite Creative maps
fortnite.ratingInProgressDesc: Our AI is evaluating this map. Check back soon.
fortnite.metaDescription: LumiKin ratings for popular Fortnite Creative maps. Find safe, fun maps for your child with scores for stranger risk, monetization pressure, and more.
fortnite.platformTagline: Player-built maps across every genre — safety and quality vary widely. Browse our ratings to find the best fits.
fortnite.noMapsYet: No maps indexed yet. Check back soon.
fortnite.scoringNote: This experience is scored on 9 dimensions adapted from the {count}-dimension LumiKin rubric. Risk category weights match the rubric (Dopamine 45%, Monetization 30%, Social 25%); per-category sub-items are aggregated into a single score.
fortnite.parentGuidePara1: Fortnite Battle Royale has unfiltered voice chat with strangers enabled by default. Go to Epic Games account settings → Parental Controls and disable voice chat, or set it to friends-only, before your child plays any online mode.
fortnite.parentGuidePara2: Fortnite is five different games in one launcher. Battle Royale, LEGO Fortnite, Festival, Rocket Racing, and Creative each have different age-appropriateness and risk profiles. Your child may start in LEGO Fortnite and drift into Battle Royale — check which modes they're actually playing.
fortnite.parentGuidePara3: V-Bucks are the shared currency across all modes. The Battle Pass, cosmetic bundles, and in-map purchases all use V-Bucks. Many Creative maps surface V-Buck spending prompts. Set spending limits in Epic Games account settings or use a prepaid card with a fixed balance.
fortnite.parentGuidePara4: Creative map content can change after our rating. Each map is rated by our AI at a point in time — creators can update maps. We recommend periodically checking which maps your child plays and watching a few minutes of gameplay with them.
fortnite.parentGuideAction: Action: Epic Games account → Parental Controls → set a PIN, disable voice chat, and cap monthly V-Buck spending. Takes under 5 minutes and significantly reduces risk across all Fortnite modes.
fortnite.metaDescVerdict: {parts}. Parent verdict and risk breakdown for {title} on Fortnite Creative.

platform.indexMetaDescription: Find rated games for kids across PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo Switch, PC, mobile, Roblox, and Fortnite Creative.
platform.indexSubtitle: Pick your child's main platform to see scored games and safest picks.
platform.editorial_playstation: PlayStation 4 and PS5 span everything from calm puzzle games to intense online multiplayer. Many great titles are available through PS Plus, but the subscription pressure and online exposure varies widely across the catalogue. LumiKin scores make it easy to find which PlayStation games are genuinely worth your child's time — and which ones come with hidden costs or engagement traps.
platform.editorial_xbox: Xbox One and Xbox Series S|X are home to hundreds of titles available through Xbox Game Pass, making it one of the most accessible platforms for families. LumiKin scores help you navigate the catalog quickly — sorting by safest picks or family co-op highlights lets you build a trusted game list without playing every title yourself.
platform.editorial_nintendo_switch: Nintendo Switch has one of the most family-friendly first-party libraries of any console, with beloved franchises like Mario, Zelda, and Pokémon consistently earning high benefit scores. The platform is also easy to manage with built-in parental controls and screen time limits. LumiKin scores help you find the standout third-party games worth adding to your collection.
platform.editorial_ios: The App Store labels many games "free", but the real cost is often attention, in-app purchases, or intrusive ads. iOS games range from genuinely educational apps to highly optimized engagement loops. LumiKin scores flag monetization pressure and dopamine-trap mechanics so you can tell the difference before downloading.
platform.editorial_android: Android gaming spans high-quality ports, indie gems, and heavily monetized free-to-play titles — often hard to tell apart from the Play Store listing alone. Google's content ratings cover subject matter, not design patterns. LumiKin scores go deeper, surfacing which games are built for benefit and which are built for maximum session length.
platform.editorial_pc: PC gaming offers the widest catalogue of any platform, from calm educational puzzlers to massive online worlds. Steam family controls and library sharing features help, but the sheer volume makes curation difficult. LumiKin scores sort the catalogue by benefit-to-risk ratio so you can build a quality family library without trial and error.
platform.noScoredExperiences: No scored experiences yet. Check back soon.

glossary.dimCognitive: How hard the game works problem-solving, planning, spatial reasoning, creativity and memory. A higher score means more of a mental workout.
glossary.dimSocial: Whether play builds teamwork, communication, empathy and ethical reasoning — usually through co-op or community features.
glossary.dimMotor: Hand-eye coordination, fine motor control and reaction time — and, in active games, real physical movement.
glossary.dimDopamine: Engagement mechanics that can tip into compulsion: variable rewards, daily streaks, FOMO events and near-miss design. A higher score means more pull to keep playing.
glossary.dimMonetization: Commercial pressure on the player — pay-to-win advantages, obscured in-game currencies, and designs that target children's spending.
glossary.dimSocialRisk: Exposure to strangers, toxic competition, and identity or status pressure in the game's social spaces.
glossary.variableRewards: Rewards given on an unpredictable schedule — sometimes you get a great drop, sometimes nothing. The same mechanic that powers slot machines; it keeps players engaged longer than fixed rewards.
glossary.streaks: Mechanics that track consecutive days of play. Skipping a day breaks the streak and forfeits progress, which pressures kids to log in even when they don’t want to.
glossary.fomo: Fear of Missing Out. Limited-time events, seasonal items, or one-off rewards that punish players for not playing right now.
glossary.payToWin: When spending real money gives a meaningful gameplay advantage — better gear, faster progression, or wins that free players can’t match.
glossary.currencyObfuscation: Using in-game currencies (gems, V-Bucks, Robux) instead of dollars, so it’s hard to tell how much something actually costs in real money.
glossary.bds: Benefits Development Score: a 0–100 rating combining cognitive, social-emotional, and motor benefits the game offers.
glossary.ris: Risk Influence Score: a 0–100 rating combining dopamine pressure, monetization pressure, and social risk in the game’s design.

bundled.kicker: Bundled online — know the live-service layer
bundled.body: The base price unlocks the singleplayer story. The connected online mode is its own product, with its own monetization and chat layer — we score those separately.
bundled.cta: Read how we handle bundled-online games →

pending.bodyTemplate: We’re still verifying this experience — scores below are provisional. Confidence: {confidence}.

browseBy.ageDek: Find what fits your child at their age — and what to wait on.
browseBy.platformDek: Switch, PlayStation, Xbox, PC, mobile — narrow by what they actually play.
browseBy.robloxDek: Inside the Roblox catalogue, scored experience by experience.
browseBy.fortniteDek: Creative islands and modes rated separately from the base game.

standfirst.body: LumiKin rates games on what they grow in a child — and what they cost in attention, money, and calm at home. Benefits come first. Every score is generated against a public rubric you can read in full, and we always name the one thing to check before you buy.

byTheNumbers.source: Every score traces to a public, version-controlled rubric.

distribution.caption: {count} games · every score on one scale
